summary: BSON inflation fails for CodeWithSpec objects containing nested RawBsonDocments
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

If C extensions are enabled, inflating a CodeWithSpec BSON object that contains a RawBsonDocument fails with the following error:


{code:java}
bson.errors.InvalidBSON: RawBSONDocument.__init__() missing 1 required positional argument: 'bson_bytes' {code}
h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

Any user trying to inflate such a CodeWithSpec BSON object.
h4. How does this affect the end user?

Inflating a CodeWithSpec BSON object with a nested RawBsonDocument does not work if C extensions are enabled.
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

Edge case, this bug has likely existed for a long time.
h4. Is this issue urgent?

Required for PYTHON-3457 & PYTHON-3458
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?

No.
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

_Does this ticket have any functional impact, or is it just test improvements?_
h3. *Acceptance Criteria*

_What specific requirements must be met to consider the design phase complete?_
{panel}

summary: There is no MacOS wheel on PyPI for Python 3.7
description:
During a conversation about changes in universal wheel support in MacOS after Python 3.7, I noticed that we aren't publishing 3.7 wheel for MacOS. I've only looked at 4.6.1, but the task will involve reviewing previous ones, too.

 
h3. Detailed steps to reproduce the problem?

_See the list of files on PyPI for 4.6.1_

_[https://pypi.org/projectIn]/pymongo/4.6.1/#files_
h3. Definition of done: what must be done to consider the task complete?
 # Wheels need to be published to PyPI (anywhere else) for existing versions.
 # [This|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-python-driver/blob/1412afef610dddf96d110517694937b2dacc5049/.evergreen/config.yml#L1199] should have an "upload release" step

h3. The operating system and version (e.g. Windows 7, OSX 10.8, ...)

MacOS

summary: Use dict instead of SON for internal command construction
description: Now that we've dropped support for Python versions older than 3.6, and dict preserves key order starting with Python 3.6, we no longer need SON. We should still keep it around to ease adoption of PyMongo 4, but we no longer need to use it ourselves. Getting rid of our usages will likely improve performance.

summary: json_util.default should always convert Int64
description:
h3. *Context*

[orjson|https://github.com/ijl/orjson] is a fast JSON encoder that could be used to speed up encoding and decoding MongoDB Extended JSON, like this:
{code:python}
>>> doc = {'a': 1, 'b': 'str', 'c': [], 'd': {}, 'e': tuple(), 'f': 1.1, 'g': float('nan'), 'h': Code('str')}
>>> orjson.dumps(doc, option=orjson.OPT_PASSTHROUGH_SUBCLASS, default=json_util.default)
b'{"a":1,"b":"str","c":[],"d":{},"e":[],"f":1.1,"g":null,"h":{"$code":"str"}}'
>>> json_util.dumps(doc)
'{"a": 1, "b": "str", "c": [], "d": {}, "e": [], "f": 1.1, "g": {"$numberDouble": "NaN"}, "h": {"$code": "str"}}'
{code}

Note that there are some slight differences in output which unfortunately means PyMongo can't use orjson by default. For example, orjson encodes nan as null which violates the Extended JSON spec.

However, this code breaks on Int64 values because json_util.default is incompatible with orjson w/ OPT_PASSTHROUGH_SUBCLASS due to Int64:
{code:python}
>>> orjson.dumps({'a': Int64(1)})
b'{"a":1}'
>>> orjson.dumps({'a': Int64(1)}, default=json_util.default)
b'{"a":1}'
>>> orjson.dumps({'a': Int64(1)}, default=json_util.default, option=orjson.OPT_PASSTHROUGH_SUBCLASS)
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
TypeError: default serializer exceeds recursion limit
{code}

h3. *Definition of done*

When encoding an Int64 in relaxed mode (the default), json_util.default should convert Int64 to a standard int so that orjson can encode it.

summary: Optimize JSON encoding of int, float, str, and None
Optimize json_util encoding performance using single dispatch table
description:
Split from PYTHON-1374.

h3. *Context*
Currently, the JSON encoder performs a chain of isinstance checks sprinkled in with a "_type_marker" lookup.  We can optimize this further by using a single dispatch table similar to how the pure python BSON encoder works:
# call type(obj) and lookup the type in the encoding table, if the type is not found continue to step 2:
# access obj._type_marker, if it exists and is one of our known types, add type(obj) to the encoding table and encode it, otherwise continue to step 3
# perform the chain of isinstance checks, if it's a subclass of the known type, add  type(obj) to the encoding table and encode it, otherwise raise TypeError.

h3. *Definition of done*
Make the above refactor and measure the performance to ensure there are no regressions and perf has improved.

summary: Preemptively cancel in progress operations when SDAM heartbeats timeout

summary: [python-bsonjs] bsonjs package shadows pymongo's bson packge
description:
Install python-bsonjs latest from source and any version of pymongo, then try to import something from bson. It will fail like this:
{code:python}
>>> from bson import encode
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "<stdin>", line 1, in <module>
ImportError: cannot import name 'encode' from 'bson' (unknown location)
{code}

bsonjs is shadowing the bson package from pymongo:
{code:python}
>>> import bson
>>> bson
<module 'bson' (<_frozen_importlib_external.NamespaceLoader object at 0x102f51550>)>
>>> dir(bson)
['__doc__', '__file__', '__loader__', '__name__', '__package__', '__path__', '__spec__', 'binary', 'codec_options', 'errors', 'objectid', 'typings', 'tz_util']
>>> bson.__spec__
ModuleSpec(name='bson', loader=<_frozen_importlib_external.NamespaceLoader object at 0x102f51550>, submodule_search_locations=_NamespacePath(['/Users/shane/git/python-bsonjs/src/bson']))
{code}

This was caused by PYTHON-3882.

summary: Pools should be properly closed instead of reset in topology
description:
{panel:bgColor=#fafbfc}
h3. *Summary*

Currently when closing servers in a topology, we clear the server's pool rather than closing it. The specification for CMAP states that pools must be closed when their owning server is closed.
h3. *Motivation*
h4. Who is the affected end user?

All end users.
h4. How does this affect the end user?

N/A, internal implementation detail.
h4. How likely is it that this problem or use case will occur?

Occurs on every client closure.
h4. If the problem does occur, what are the consequences and how severe are they?

Non-conformance with the CMAP spec.
h4. Is this issue urgent?

Required for CMAP standardized logging.
h4. Is this ticket required by a downstream team?

No.
h4. Is this ticket only for tests?

Yes.

_Does this ticket have any functional impact, or is it just test improvements?_
h3. *Acceptance Criteria*

_What specific requirements must be met to consider the design phase complete?_
{panel}

summary: Allow UUID key_id to be passed to ClientEncryption.encrypt
Optimize JSON decoding performance by avoiding object_pairs_hook
description:
[ilukyanchikov|https://github.com/ilukyanchikov] has created [PR #1493: PYTHON-1374: Optimized performance by removing object_pairs_hook call if we need the default conversion to dict behavior|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-python-driver/pull/1493] in [mongo-python-driver|https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-python-driver]

_Issue Text:_
Optimized performance by removing object_pairs_hook call if we need the default conversion to dict behavior [jira task](https://jira.mongodb.org/projects/PYTHON/issues/PYTHON-1374)
In object_hook, a dictionary is already passed, and calling object_pairs_hook for casting pairs to dict is redundant.
I validated my changes with test_json_util cases. `pytest -v -s test/test_json_util.py`
Compare performance with TestJson*Decoding cases(changes affect performance only in these cases) `pytest -v -s test/performance/perf_test.py::TestJsonFlatDecoding test/performance/perf_test.py::TestJsonDeepDecoding test/performance/perf_test.py::TestJsonFullDecoding`

| Test Name        | master | current branch        |
|------------------|------------------|--------------------|
| JsonFlatDecoding | 96.18636555725531| 117.50816018425552 |
| JsonDeepDecoding | 58.337081713027956| 77.52136671585596 |
| JsonFullDecoding | 33.81260536995342| 38.88821810950474  |

This fix only works with JSONOptions where document_class is dict, and I haven't found cases where something other than dict was used.

summary: Optimize JSON decoding using set intersection and lookup table
description:
Split from PYTHON-1374:

bq. Use set intersection to quickly find the $ key (if any) for a BSON type in json_util.object_hook.


We can optimize JSON decoding using set intersection and a lookup table for better json_util.loads() performance.

description:

[https://github.com/mongodb-labs/full-stack-fastapi-mongodb/issues/23]

I'm receiving the following error during the static page generation for /, reset-password, and login when running 'docker compose build':

summary: Langchain similarity search showing the vector embeddings in the result
Langchain - Local caching leading to results being missed
description:
When using LangChain-mongoDB
There is Cache miss on semantically similar terms like

 

Ex: queries like "help me watch a movie set in summer" . "help me see movies set in summer" are not returning cached result

 

A cause could be due to interference from local cache optimization.

 
{noformat}
 

from langchain_mongodb.cache import MongoDBAtlasSemanticCache 
set_llm_cache() 
semantic_cache = MongoDBAtlasSemanticCache( connection_string=ATLAS_CONNECTION_STRING, embedding=embeddings, collection_name="semantic_cache", database_name="langchain_chatbot", wait_until_ready=True # Optional, waits until the cache is ready to be used ) {noformat}

summary: Allow passing arbitrary options to create_search_index models
description:
h3. *Context*
We should allow passing arbitrary options to create_search_index models. This will make create_search_index/create_search_indexes more future proof. For example with this change users would have been able to create vectorSearch type indexes without waiting for PYTHON-4208.

h3. *Definition of done*
The following code should send newField in the createSearchIndexes command:
{code:python}
coll.create_search_index(model={"definition": {...}, "newField": 1})
{code}

summary: MONGODB-AWS credential caching no longer works
description:
The changes in PYTHON-4260 broke MONGODB-AWS credential caching. One issue is that set_use_cached_credentials() is no longer called. Another is that we try to call pymongo_auth_aws.set_cached_credentials which doesn't exist:
{code}
 [2024/03/26 12:59:10.177]         except Exception:
 [2024/03/26 12:59:10.177]             # Clear the cached credentials if we hit a failure in auth.
 [2024/03/26 12:59:10.177] >           pymongo_auth_aws.set_cached_credentials(None)
 [2024/03/26 12:59:10.177] E           AttributeError: module 'pymongo_auth_aws' has no attribute 'set_cached_credentials'
 [2024/03/26 12:59:10.177] pymongo/auth_aws.py:97: AttributeError
{code}
https://spruce.mongodb.com/task/mongo_python_driver_aws_auth_test_mac__platform~macos_1014_aws_auth_test_7.0_f757fe39cc12520e299090adf750fc07eb03dec8_24_03_26_17_18_54/logs?execution=0&sortBy=STATUS&sortDir=ASC

summary: list_search_indexes does not forward comment argument
Out-of-bounds read when decoding invalid bson
description:
*CVE ID:*

CVE-2024-5629

*Title:*
Out-of-bounds read in bson module of PyMongo

*Description:*
An out-of-bounds read in the 'bson' module of PyMongo 4.6.2 or earlier allows deserialization of malformed BSON provided by a Server to raise an exception which may contain arbitrary application memory.

*CVSS Score: 4.7*

[https://www.first.org/cvss/calculator/3.1#CVSS:3.1/AV:N/AC:H/PR:N/UI:R/S:C/C:L/I:N/A:L]

*List all affected product versions:*
MongoDB PyMongo versions prior to and including 4.6.2

*CWE:*
CWE-125: Out-of-bounds Read

*Is a fixed version available:*
4.6.3

*How was the issue found, Internally/Externally:*
Externally

summary: Raise informative error when server_description.round_trip_time is None in server selection
description:
[https://github.com/mongodb/mongo-python-driver/pull/1361] outlines an unexpected error case where a server description is in an unknown state but is attempting to access its round_trip_time. This behavior should result in a more useful error that contains the server description states.

Here's an example:
{code:python}

summary: Issue loading the dns.resolver module with pymongo 4.7.0
description:
h3. Detailed steps to reproduce the problem?

_The email-validator.validate_email ([https://github.com/JoshData/python-email-validator/blob/main/email_validator/validate_email.py]) call is failing after the pymongo update to 4.7.0._

_When we debugged the cause for this we found out that there is an issue in loading "dns.resolver" module._

_You can easily reproduce in REPL._

_Below code gives the error "_

{color:#de350b}AttributeError: module 'dns' has no attribute 'resolver'{color}"

 
{code:java}
from pymongo.database import Database
import dns.resolver
dns_resolver = dns.resolver.get_default_resolver()
{code}
 

Where as below code works fine

 
{code:java}
import dns.resolver
dns_resolver = dns.resolver.get_default_resolver()
{code}
 

Even this code works fine (Here we imported pymongo after dns import)

 
{code:java}
import dns.resolver
from pymongo.database import Database
dns_resolver = dns.resolver.get_default_resolver()
{code}
 
h3. Definition of done: what must be done to consider the task complete?

The below code must pass without any error

 
{code:java}
from pymongo.database import Database
import dns.resolver
dns_resolver = dns.resolver.get_default_resolver()
{code}
 
h3. The exact Python version used, with patch level:

{{$ python -c "import sys; print(sys.version)"}}

3.12.2 (main, Feb  6 2024, 20:19:44) [Clang 15.0.0 (clang-1500.1.0.2.5)]
h3. The exact version of PyMongo used, with patch level:

{{$ python -c "import pymongo; print(pymongo.version); print(pymongo.has_c())"}}

4.7.0

True
h3. Describe how MongoDB is set up. Local vs Hosted, version, topology, load balanced, etc.
h3. The operating system and version (e.g. Windows 7, OSX 10.8, ...)
h3. Web framework or asynchronous network library used, if any, with version (e.g. Django 1.7, mod_wsgi 4.3.0, gevent 1.0.1, Tornado 4.0.2, ...)
h3. *Security Vulnerabilities*

If you’ve identified a security vulnerability in a driver or any other MongoDB project, please report it according to the instructions [here|https://www.mongodb.com/docs/manual/tutorial/create-a-vulnerability-report/]

[pymongocrypt] Cannot build sdist with Setuptools 70.0
description:
h3. Detailed steps to reproduce the problem?
Attempt to install pymongocrypt from source.

{{configuration error: `tool.setuptools.dynamic.version` must be valid exactly by one definition (0 matches found)}}.

Setuptools version 70.0 introduced new validation, that fails with our {{tool.setuptools.dynamic.version}}.  We have to change it to {{pymongocrypt.version.__version__}} instead of {{pymongocrypt/version.__version__}}.

https://spruce.mongodb.com/task/mongo_python_driver_tests_python_version_rhel8_test_encryption__platform~rhel8_auth_ssl~noauth_nossl_python_version~3.10_encryption~encryption_crypt_shared_test_5.0_replica_set_patch_594dacaaf969904ba938bd29b28e40579d418673_664ca81aaf0a1e00077c77d0_24_05_21_13_56_46/logs?execution=0&sortBy=STATUS&sortDir=ASC

summary: Pymongo import is extremely slow on windows
description:
h3. Detailed steps to reproduce the problem?

It takes more than 500ms to import pymongo on windows 11.

`python -c "import time; start = time.perf_counter(); import pymongo; print(time.perf_counter() - start)"`
0.5733743999153376

 

Generating an import trace: (visualisation attached)

`python -X importtime -c "import pymongo; print(pymongo.__version__)" 2> imports.log`
h3. Definition of done: what must be done to consider the task complete?

Should not take more than 100ms just to import pymongo
h3. The exact Python version used, with patch level:

{{$ python -c "import sys; print(sys.version)"}}

3.12.0 (tags/v3.12.0:0fb18b0, Oct  2 2023, 13:03:39) [MSC v.1935 64 bit (AMD64)]
h3. The exact version of PyMongo used, with patch level:

{{$ python -c "import pymongo; print(pymongo.version); print(pymongo.has_c())"}}

4.7.2
True
h3. The operating system and version (e.g. Windows 7, OSX 10.8, ...)

Windows 11

summary: Optimize find/aggregate/command by avoiding duplicate calls to _get_topology
description:
Optimize find/aggregate/command by avoiding duplicate calls to _get_topology. The read and database.command() code paths accidentally call _get_topology() twice. _get_topology is expensive since it requires locks and checking the state of running threads. 

Related to PYTHON-4347.

summary: Lazy loading modules is not thread-safe
description:
h3. *Context*

[https://github.com/python/cpython/issues/114763] uncovered that lazily-loaded modules cannot have their attributes accessed in a thread-safe manner. This was fixed in Python 3.11.9 and Python 3.12.  
h3. *Definition of done*

Our lazy-loading code should check to verify that the user is using a thread-safe version of Python before running.

*Pitfalls*

{color:#505f79}N/A.{color}

summary: Don't Print the value of senstive subtype bytes in the repr
Improve performance of session pool
description:
h3. *Context*
The benchmark in PYTHON-4481 shows a lot of time spent waiting in get_server_session and return_server_session. I believe this is due to contention overhead and synchronization cost on the Topology lock. We should be able to make the session pool thread safe and lock-free to avoid this bottleneck.

h3. *Definition of done*
Lock-free and thread-safe session pool. Verify this results in a performance improvement.
